proposal.working_title: AI Implementation Courses Explained: How Mid-Market Operators Pick Training That Ships Real Workflows
proposal.one_line_angle: Most AI training spend ends in slideware because leaders compare courses on hours and price rather than on shipped workflow output; here is the operator scorecard mid-market teams use to pick a program that produces a working build, not just a completion certificate.
outline_bullets: What 'operator-grade' AI courses actually deliver vs lecture-heavy curricula, and the four signals that separate training that ships workflows from training that produces decks

The scorecard mid-market leaders use before purchasing: build-hours-to-lecture-hours ratio, instructor track record on similar stacks, deliverable artifacts at completion, and post-course implementation support

How to evaluate ROI before you commit budget: alumni outcomes, integration depth with your existing tooling, and the three diagnostic questions that surface a course's real fit for a done-for-you operator
proposal.cannibalization_rationale: no overlap detected against current inventory
